title: centaurea decipiens subsp. decipiens
intro: Centaurea decipiens subsp. decipiens, also known as lesser knapweed, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Centaurea decipiens subsp. decipiens include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
